note: mock_rate encodes the IDEAL outcome so `--decider mock` demonstrates the pipeline; real deciders (api/claude-cli) ignore it and measure the description. Negatives are near-misses against sibling sec-* scanners that share 'security' vocab but produce a different artifact than a STRIDE threat model.

queries.text: threat model the new payments service
run a STRIDE analysis on our OAuth login flow before the design review
we're adding a webhook receiver in src/webhooks/ — what can go wrong from a security standpoint? map the trust boundaries
my manager wants a security architecture review of the multi-tenant data layer for the design doc
can you enumerate the attack surface for the file-upload feature and list threats by spoofing/tampering/etc?
produce a threat-model doc for the whole app covering assets, data flows, and residual risk per category
we're integrating a third-party billing API — walk the data flows and identify elevation-of-privilege and info-disclosure risks
stride threat modelling for the notification microservice pls, need it for tomorrow's arch review
before we ship the new SSO integration, model the repudiation and DoS threats around the token exchange
run a full OWASP Top 10 security scan of the whole codebase before prod
queries.note: comprehensive codebase audit → sec-scan, not a STRIDE model

queries.text: generate a pentest checklist for the customer portal so I can hand it to the external assessor
check our infra/terraform/ for exposed endpoints and over-permissioned IAM roles
do an ASVS L2 compliance gap analysis and give me the control matrix
audit package.json and requirements.txt for known CVEs and unmaintained deps
scan the repo and git history for leaked API keys and credentials
review our GitHub Actions workflows for supply-chain risks and pinned actions
quick security gate on my staged diff before I push this auth change
explain how the trust boundary between the gateway and the worker is enforced in this code
queries.note: understand existing code, not model new threats → explain

queries.text: before the design review of the new realtime sync feature, do a STRIDE threat model — I don't want a full codebase scan yet, just the attack surface and trust boundaries
queries.note: shares 'security'/'scan' vocab but explicitly wants a design-time STRIDE model, not the full audit → must beat sec-scan

queries.text: map the assets and threats-by-category for the API before we scope the pentest
queries.note: mentions pentest but the ask is to model threats/assets first → must beat sec-pentest

queries.text: generate the tailored pentest checklist for the login and payment flows for our red team engagement
queries.note: deliverable is a testing checklist for an assessor → must lose to sec-pentest

queries.text: do a comprehensive OWASP Top 10 audit across the whole service including secrets, deps, and static analysis before the acquisition review
queries.note: full multi-scanner codebase audit → must lose to sec-scan
